mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which compresses input costs and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or negative returns occur in more than 70% of acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 3.0%, persistent federal funds rate hikes force financial conditions to tighten aggressively, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting negative returns, or positive returns dominate when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate profit pools and improves forward earnings multiples, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero correlation (Rank IC = 0.00) with 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, or negative returns occur in more than 60% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150,000 confirm robust economic momentum and consumer wage growth, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 150k threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (50%) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production declines while retail sales rise indicates inventory drawdowns and supply chain bottlenecks, which raise input costs and compress margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with a coefficient significant at the 95% confidence level, or positive returns occur in more than 65% of divergence episodes over a 10-year sample.
